hypothesis: Participants with higher risk aversion—operationalized as a greater average number of safe choices across the Probability Variation (PV) and Risk Variation (RV) tasks—will exhibit fewer decision errors—operationalized as a lower count of stochastic dominance violations in the Lottery Variation (LV) task.

data_plan.source_type: Laboratory experiment dataset (multiple price list risk tasks)
data_plan.wave_or_subset: Full dataset with exclusion of two non-participants (age==99). Tasks analyzed: PV, RV, LV.
data_plan.sample_size: 106 (after excluding 2 rows with age==99 from 108 total)
data_plan.access_details: Local file path inside container: /app/data/RiskData.dta. Context/source links in proposed_analysis.pdf: original article https://osf.io/2efb6/ and original data https://osf.io/8xq95/
data_plan.notes: Variables: Risk1x and Risk2x indicate safe choice in PV and RV (1=safe, 0=risky). Risk3x indicates selection of RV lottery in LV (1=RV, 0=PV). In LV, item 5 has identical options and is excluded from error counts. Count variables have many ties; Task 1 uses Kendall's tau; Task 2 uses Pearson. IO paths standardized to /app/data and /app/artifacts.

steps: Load Stata dataset from /app/data/RiskData.dta into pandas.
Exclude non-participants by filtering out rows with age==99.
Construct PV safe-choice sum: risk1_sum = sum of Risk11..Risk110 (1=safe, 0=risky). Validate presence of all columns.
Construct RV safe-choice sum: risk2_sum = sum of Risk21..Risk210 (1=safe, 0=risky). Validate presence of all columns.
Compute risk aversion measure: risk_avg_safe = (risk1_sum + risk2_sum) / 2.

Construct LV decision error count (violations of stochastic dominance) as: decision_errors_lv = (1 - Risk31) + (1 - Risk32) + (1 - Risk33) + (1 - Risk34) + Risk36 + Risk37 + Risk38 + Risk39 + Risk310. Exclude LV item 5.
Drop rows with missing values in risk_avg_safe or decision_errors_lv.

Primary analysis (Task 1): Compute Kendall's tau-b between risk_avg_safe and decision_errors_lv; compute one-sided p-value for negative association; generate 95% bootstrap CI (e.g., 2000 resamples, fixed seed for reproducibility).
Supplementary robustness (Task 1): Repeat Kendall's tau-b using risk1_sum and decision_errors_lv; and using risk2_sum and decision_errors_lv.
Constraint analysis (Task 2): Compute Pearson correlation between risk_avg_safe and decision_errors_lv; compute one-sided p-value for negative association; compute 95% CI via Fisher z-transformation.
Diagnostics/visualization: Create a scatter/count plot of risk_avg_safe vs decision_errors_lv with LOWESS smoother; save to /app/artifacts.
Write numeric results to /app/artifacts/results.json and a compact CSV table to /app/artifacts/table_correlations.csv; print summary to stdout.

models.planned_estimation: Kendall's tau-b between risk_avg_safe and decision_errors_lv; 95% bootstrap CI (e.g., 2000 resamples).
models.inference_criteria: One-sided test for negative association (tau < 0) at alpha=0.05; report two-sided p, one-sided p, and CI.
models.qualification: Kendall's tau-b is suitable for discrete count variables with many ties and non-normality, providing a conservative test of monotonic association. This addresses distributional features in the data while testing the same substantive claim.

models.planned_estimation: Pearson correlation between risk_avg_safe and decision_errors_lv; 95% CI via Fisher z.
models.inference_criteria: One-sided test for negative association (r < 0) at alpha=0.05; report two-sided p, one-sided p, and CI.
models.qualification: Aligns with the original paper’s reported Pearson results and meets the constraint of not using rank-order tests.

variables.description: Risk aversion measure: average number of safe choices across PV and RV tasks; risk_avg_safe = (risk1_sum + risk2_sum)/2 where risk1_sum = sum(Risk11..Risk110) and risk2_sum = sum(Risk21..Risk210), each Riskij is 1 if safe option chosen and 0 otherwise.
variables.qualification: Matches the original operationalization using PV and RV safe choices as a proxy for risk aversion.
variables.transformation: Summation of 10 binary items per task; arithmetic mean across tasks.
variables.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion if any Risk1x or Risk2x items required for sums are missing.

variables.description: Decision error count in LV task: violations of stochastic dominance across nine eligible LV items (excluding item 5). Computed as decision_errors_lv = (1-Risk31) + (1-Risk32) + (1-Risk33) + (1-Risk34) + Risk36 + Risk37 + Risk38 + Risk39 + Risk310.
variables.qualification: Follows the LV design: RV dominates PV in items 1–4; PV dominates RV in items 6–10; item 5 identical and excluded.
variables.transformation: Linear combination of LV item indicators; exclusion of item 5.
variables.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion if any required Risk3x item is missing.

variables.description: PV task safe-choice sum: risk1_sum = Risk11 + ... + Risk110 (1=safe, 0=risky). Used in robustness tests.
variables.qualification: Provides within-task association check for PV choices.
variables.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion if any required item is missing.

variables.description: RV task safe-choice sum: risk2_sum = Risk21 + ... + Risk210 (1=safe, 0=risky). Used in robustness tests.
variables.qualification: Provides within-task association check for RV choices.
variables.missing_data_handling: Listwise deletion if any required item is missing.

variables.description: Exclusion variable: age; used to drop observations with age==99 (non-participants) prior to analysis.
variables.qualification: Ensures analytic sample reflects actual participants as noted by original authors.
variables.missing_data_handling: If age is missing, retain unless other variables are missing per listwise rule.

planned_method.tools_software: Python 3.11; packages: pandas, scipy, statsmodels, matplotlib, seaborn, numpy.

files.data/analysis_entrypoint.py: Main Python script to run the proposed analysis. Reads /app/data/RiskData.dta, applies age filter (age!=99), constructs risk1_sum, risk2_sum, risk_avg_safe, decision_errors_lv; computes Kendall's tau-b (with bootstrap CI) and Pearson r (with Fisher z CI); generates scatter/LOWESS plot; writes outputs to /app/artifacts.

codebase.notes: The script seeds NumPy RNG (12345) for bootstrap reproducibility. Ensure the dataset is available at /app/data/RiskData.dta and that /app/artifacts exists or is creatable. No R execution is required.
